LAW AND MOTION 302, RULING - THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANT JOSE ZELAYA'S MOTION TO STRIKE PORTIONS OF PLAINTIFF FELIX G. CALLE FLORES' SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT IS GRANTED. THE THIRD CAUSE OF ACTION FOR INTENTIONAL TORT IS STRICKEN. PLAINTIFF DID NOT OBTAIN LEAVE OF COURT TO ADD A CAUSE OF ACTION TO THE COMPLAINT. THE EXEMPLARY DAMAGES ATTACHMENT IS ALSO STRICKEN, AS A NEGLIGENCE CLAIM CANNOT SUPPORT A PRAYER OF PUNITIVE DAMAGES. IF PLAINTIFF WANTS TO ADD A CLAIM FOR INTENTIONAL TORT, PLAINTIFF MUST FILE A MOTION SEEKING LEAVE OF COURT. (SEE CCP SEC. 472, PROVIDING THAT ANY PLEADING MAY BE AMENDED ONLY ONCE WITHOUT LEAVE OF COURT.) ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; REPORTER: SHERYL L. ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Dec-03-2014 Continued To Jan-21-15 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Defendant Jose Zelaya'S Motion To Strike Portions Of Plaintiff Felix G. Calle Flores' Second Amended Complaint
Off Calendar Nov-04-2014 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES - ATTY. TAMARA COOL OF THE LAW OFFICES OF OLIVER J. VASQUEZ, TEL. NO. (213)629-1089 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT BY TELEPHONE APPEARANCE;
NO OTHER APPEARANCES ON THE MATTER.

RULING - The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT JOSE ZELAYA'S Motion To Strike Portions Of Plaintiff Felix G. Calle Flores' Second Amended Complaint is Granted. The third cause of action for Intentional Tort is stricken. Plaintiff did not obtain leave of court to add a cause of action to the complaint. The exemplary damages attachment is also stricken, as a negligence claim cannot support a prayer of punitive damages. If plaintiff wants to add a claim for Intentional Tort, plaintiff must file a motion seeking leave of court. (See CCP sec. 472, providing that any pleading may be amended only once without leave of court.) ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Sheryl L. Sawyer, CSR # 5976 =(302/EHG)

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar May-21-2014 Continued To Jun-23-14 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES: No appearance on this matter. RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT JOSE ZELAYA's DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is continued to June 23, 2014 to give Defendant Zelaya the opportunity to comply with Local Rule 2.6B and 2.6C. Defendant Zelaya must deliver courtesy copies of the moving papers and operative pleading to Department 302, with a cover letter stating the new hearing date, no later than May 21, 2014 at 4:00 pm or the demurrer will be overruled. Any exhibit must be separated by an exhibit tab per CRC 3.1110(f). Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Not reported.
